name: Chicken Soup#2
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the chicken pieces and place them in a large stockpot. Cover with water and bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer until the chicken falls off the bones (approximately 2-3 hours).
3. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and let it cool.
4. When cool enough to handle, remove the bones and skin, and cut all the chicken into small pieces, being careful not to leave any bones.
5. Add the chicken to the stock left in the pan and refrigerate overnight.
6. When you are ready to serve, skim off the grease from the top of the stock (the chicken will be very moist from having remained in the stock overnight).
7. Return the pot to heat and slowly bring it to a boil.
8. Add onions, celery, carrots, tomatoes, cabbage, and potatoes.
9. Add bouillon cubes, bay leaf, salt, pepper, marjoram, basil, thyme, and parsley.
10. Simmer until the vegetables are done to your liking.
11. Then add the broken pieces of spaghetti and cook until tender. The soup will be very thick, almost stew-like.
12. It freezes well.
